evals.name: vendor-by-github-shorthand
evals.prompt: I'm building a TypeScript validation library and I want to study Zod's tests as a reference. The repo is colinhacks/zod. Can you vendor it into this project so I can grep through the source offline without hitting GitHub? I don't need the docs or example folders, just the source.
expected_behavior: Invokes the ingraft CLI via bunx ingraft@latest (not a local scripts/vendor.ts)
Uses the `add` subcommand with the colinhacks/zod GitHub shorthand
Applies --exclude-dir for docs and examples, or filters in the same spirit
Does not modify files under vendor/zod manually
Does not invent flags that don't exist in the CLI

evals.name: scan-package-manifest-for-candidates
evals.prompt: This project has a bunch of npm dependencies. I'd like to know which of them have public source repos that I could vendor for offline reference, and then pick a couple to add. Can you figure that out and walk me through it?
expected_behavior: Runs `bunx ingraft@latest` with no subcommand, or `bunx ingraft@latest deps`, to scan package manifests
Does not write a custom script to scan package.json — defers to the CLI
Surfaces the matched repos and asks which to vendor before mutating the project
Uses the subtree strategy by default unless the user requests otherwise

evals.name: list-and-doctor-status
evals.prompt: What's already vendored in this project, and is the vendoring setup healthy? I want to know if the editor ignore files and tool ignore files are in sync with reality.
expected_behavior: Runs `bunx ingraft@latest list` to show vendored repos and strategies
Runs `bunx ingraft@latest doctor` to check ignore/tooling sync
Does not read vendor/ contents directly to enumerate repos — uses the CLI
Reports the strategy (subtree, submodule, clone-ignore) for each entry

evals.name: destructive-history-rewrite
evals.prompt: I committed the effect vendor folder yesterday with some fixture files that contain real API keys. I need them completely gone from git history, not just deleted in HEAD. How do I do this with ingraft?
expected_behavior: Identifies `remove --dangerously-rewrite-history` as the correct command
Walks the user through the pre-flight checklist (git-filter-repo installed, clean working tree, SHA rewrite implications, force-push coordination)
Does NOT run the command without explicit user confirmation
Warns that collaborators must re-clone or rebase after the rewrite

evals.name: negative-not-a-vendoring-task
evals.prompt: Can you write me a quick bash script that downloads the latest zod release tarball from npm and unpacks it into ./tmp/ for inspection? Just a one-off, not part of the build.
expected_behavior: Does NOT invoke ingraft — this is a one-off tarball download, not vendoring into the project
Writes a short shell script (curl/tar or npm pack) without consulting the skill
If the agent does load the skill, it recognizes this is out of scope and falls back to writing the script
